title: Executive Coercion of Platforms to Suppress Lawful Political Dissent

violations.source: Article V Section 2.1 and Section 2.6; Article XIII Section 2.0.5
violations.summary: Senior executive officials pressure major digital platforms through threatened regulatory retaliation to suppress lawful political dissent critical of the administration without issuing a formal order. The Constitution does not permit the executive to accomplish through coercive private pressure what Article V forbids it to do directly, and Article XIII Section 2.0.5 bars use of agency power for political retaliation.

satisfied_duties.duty: determine on expedited review whether the state-coerced suppression is attributable to government action and enjoin any unconstitutional pressure
satisfied_duties.outcome: held that the pressure campaign constituted state action through coercive use of threatened agency power, enjoined further retaliation, and ordered restoration of access where the suppression resulted from that coercion
satisfied_duties.source: Article V Section 2.1 and Section 2.6; Article XIII Section 2.0.5

satisfied_duties.duty: investigate whether executive officials used agency threats or regulatory leverage to induce viewpoint-based suppression
satisfied_duties.outcome: found that executive officials used threatened regulatory retaliation to induce viewpoint-based suppression and referred the responsible officials for prosecution or removal proceedings
satisfied_duties.source: Article XIII Section 2.0.5 and Article XII

ai_summary.primary_violation: Senior executive officials pressure major digital platforms through threatened regulatory retaliation to suppress lawful political dissent critical of the administration without issuing a formal order. The Constitution does not permit the executive to accomplish through coercive private pressure what Article V forbids it to do directly, and Article XIII Section 2.0.5 bars use of agency power for political retaliation.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
